_meta.purpose: Canonical signal vocabulary. Every contraindications, physical_contraindications, and safety trigger_signals field in the project joins on these IDs. No synonyms. lowercase_snake_case. One concept per ID.
_meta.corpus_tags_vs_signal_ids: memory_corpus.json records use free-form retrieval tags (e.g. 'burnout_signal', 'injury_active', 'high_anxiety') that are NOT signal IDs. Signal IDs govern contraindication checks and safety routing. Corpus tags govern retrieval scoring. The scenario_state_mapping section below bridges the two where they overlap.

scenario_state_mapping.note: Maps episode scenario_state boolean keys to canonical signal IDs for agent use. When a boolean is true, treat the mapped signal ID as active for contraindication and routing checks.

signals.description: User-reported or system-inferred energy at or below actionable threshold (approx. 3/10). Contraindication for high-intensity exercise. Maps to scenario_state.low_energy=true.
Acute sleep deprivation — less than 5 hours reported or inferred. Contraindication for high-intensity exercise. Maps to scenario_state.sleep_deprived=true.
User with a chronic condition reports a symptom flare today. Activity scope should be reduced to lowest-barrier options regardless of baseline fitness.
Generic active acute physical injury. Broad contraindication for HIIT and high-impact activities. Use specific variants (acute_knee_injury, acute_injury_lower_limb) when body-part is known.
Active knee injury. Contraindication for walking >5min, yoga with standing poses, cycling, and lower-body weight-bearing exercises. Matches user_athlete_injured physical_contraindications.
Active lower-limb injury requiring no weight-bearing. Broader than acute_knee_injury — includes ankle, hip, foot injuries. Contraindication for all weight-bearing movement.
Any activity requiring meaningful lower-limb load-bearing (walking, running, jumping, standing yoga poses). Canonical profile contraindication used for knee-injury recovery cases.

User has disclosed a chronic illness (e.g. fibromyalgia, MS, IBD, diabetes) that requires exercise and nutrition modifications. Does not specify which illness — use with flare_day for active episodes.

User has disclosed or system has inferred clinically severe depression. Contraindication for HIIT and high-intensity standalone sessions. Requires evidence-based low-intensity + BA first-line approach per NICE NG222.
User reports difficulty initiating, planning, or sequencing tasks. Indicates need for decompose_to_microstep tool and single-action plans. Common in ADHD, burnout, severe depression.
Reduced concentration or attention capacity reported. Plans should be concrete, short, and low-decision. Avoid open-ended or multi-step sequences.
Acute cognitive load — exam week, major deadline, information overload. Plans must be under 30 min total and require minimal mental effort. Maps to scenario_state.exam_period=true.

User reports or describes a panic attack (racing heart, shortness of breath, acute fear). Grounding and somatic techniques (5-4-3-2-1, box breathing) are first response. Distinguish from anger — see object_directed_anger_safe.
Generalized acute emotional distress not classified as panic or crisis. Requires soft check-in before plan generation. May escalate to T1 if hopelessness language follows.

Elevated anxiety state reported by user or inferred from journal/mood logs. Distinct from panic_episode (acute) and passive_hopelessness (T1 safety signal). Maps to memory_corpus tag 'high_anxiety'. Guides recommendation toward grounding, somatic, and low-stimulation activities.
User reports loss of interest or pleasure in previously enjoyed activities. NICE NG222 first-line signal for behavioural activation (BA). Do not prescribe high-intensity exercise as a mood fix.

User expresses generalised hopelessness without specific suicidal ideation ('nothing helps', 'what's even the point'). C-SSRS Tier 1 trigger. Requires soft check-in; does NOT require emergency bypass alone.

User expresses profound exhaustion as an identity or persistent state rather than a temporary tiredness ('I'm always exhausted', 'I'm running on empty'). C-SSRS Tier 1 co-trigger when combined with passive_hopelessness.

User expresses existential futility ('why do I bother', 'what's even the point of any of this'). C-SSRS Tier 1 trigger. Requires disambiguation — may reflect burnout, not SI. Frequency rule applies: >=2 occurrences in session promotes to T2.

User expresses active thoughts of self-harm or suicide without a specific method, plan, or stated intent. C-SSRS Tier 2. Requires safety planning resources and 988/crisis line. Does NOT halt plan generation unless frequency escalation triggers.
User discloses a specific self-harm plan (e.g. method + timeframe). C-SSRS Tier 3 trigger. Immediately halts all plan generation. Emergency protocol activates.
User discloses both a method and intent to act. C-SSRS Tier 3 trigger. Single utterance is sufficient — no frequency threshold required. Emergency protocol activates.

User language indicates imminent behavioral preparation ('I have the pills', 'I'm going to do it tonight', 'I've already decided'). C-SSRS Tier 3 trigger. Emergency protocol activates regardless of prior tier.

SAFE SIGNAL — anger directed at an object, situation, or third party, NOT at self. Must NOT trigger T2 or T3. Examples: 'I want to punch a wall', 'I could throttle my boss', 'I'm going to kill this laptop'. Disambiguation rule: if subject of harm is not the user's own body or life, treat as object_directed_anger_safe.
User's active habit streak has been broken. Variable-reward calibration required — do not respond with generic or punitive language. Applies to streak_progress.current_streak_days dropping to 0.

An established wellness routine has been significantly disrupted by external circumstances (illness, schedule change, life event). Stale plan retirement likely needed. Maps to scenario_state or memory_corpus stale entries.

Pattern of repeatedly not completing planned exercise sessions (2+ missed sessions in recent memory). NICE NG222 BA approach warranted — start smaller, not harder. Check rejected_activities_memory for prior failures.

User reports withdrawal from social contact or significant reduction in social interaction. CDC stress guidance supports social connection as a mental health protective factor. Surface social habits and community challenges.
Primary caregiver or support person is unavailable for this session or period. Affects planning for users (e.g. new_parent) who depend on partner availability for extended routine windows.
User has disclosed LGBTQ+ identity in session. Requires culturally affirming, identity-safe response framing. Minority stress model applies. Do not treat disclosure as a wellness problem to solve.
Signals consistent with the minority stress model — chronic stress arising from stigma, discrimination, or identity-based social pressure. Affects baseline stress load and wellness plan framing.

Professional or caregiver burnout indicators present: emotional exhaustion, reduced efficacy, cynicism about activities. Maps to user_professional_burnout persona and WEEKDAY_HIGH_STRESS context. Requires short-session, low-commitment planning.

User is pregnant. Exercise and nutrition recommendations require significant modification. High-intensity and contact sports contraindicated. Always defer to healthcare provider for exercise prescription.

User is in the postpartum period. Maps to user_new_parent_fatigued persona. Sleep deprivation is structural. Ultra-low-effort micro-habits are the appropriate entry point. Pelvic floor and core contraindications may apply.

User is 65 or older. WHO PA 2020 guidelines have specific recommendations for older adults including balance training. Exercise intensity and duration adjustments apply. Fall-risk contraindications relevant.
User is under 18. Safeguarding protocols apply. Crisis routing may require different resources (youth crisis lines). Parental/guardian notification rules may apply depending on jurisdiction.
